entities.name: Apolin
entities.raw_quote: O King Apolin, great, powerful, and venerable, I, your servant...

entities.name: Maraloth
entities.raw_quote: ...Apolin, Maraloth, Berith, I, so-and-so, exorcise and conjure you...

entities.name: Berith
entities.raw_quote: ...Apolin, Maraloth, Berith, I, so-and-so, exorcise and conjure you...

entities.name: master spirit (unspecified name)
entities.raw_quote: ...a certain master, whom the three kings will command... ‘Behold, we give you what you have many times requested.’

entities.name: Sathan
entities.raw_quote: ...O Apolin, Maraloch, Berith, Sathan, Beliath, Belzebuc, [and] Lucifer...

entities.name: sword

attributes.usage: placed under head during sleep; claimed and returned by master

entities.raw_quote: ...the sword, which the demonic teacher claims at the beginning of the lessons but relinquishes to the magician at the end...

entities.name: circle

attributes.form: single band inscribed within a square
attributes.elements: seven spirits’ names, four cardinal directions, student/master labels

entities.raw_quote: The circle required for this first experiment is a single band inscribed within a square. Inscribed in and around the band are the names of seven spirits...

entities.name: blood (bird)

attributes.use: writing spirit’s name and magician’s name in circle/cloth

entities.raw_quote: ...write it with the aforementioned blood... If the blood of one bird is not enough, you may kill as many as you wish.

entities.name: cloth bundle

attributes.function: pillow during sleep; contains sword; used for grounding ritual space

entities.raw_quote: ...put down the sword and wrap it in the aforesaid cloth, and, having made a bundle, lie down on it...

entities.name: tablet
entities.raw_quote: ...you must have a tablet, and must write down all that he tells you.

entities.name: Cesarius of Heisterbach story (schoolboy)
entities.raw_quote: In the church of Saint Simeon in the diocese of Trier there was a little schoolboy... ‘If you pay homage to me, I will compose the verses for you.’

entities.name: ars notoria

attributes.contrast: uses angelic invocation vs. demonic in Munich Handbook

entities.raw_quote: ...the later medieval magic of the so-called ars notoria, which uses prayers and invocation of angels to gain mastery of the liberal and mechanical arts.

summary: This chunk details a necromantic ritual from the Munich Handbook (CLM 849) for acquiring knowledge of all sciences through a demonic master spirit conjured by three kings (Apolin, Maraloth, Berith). It outlines preparation involving sleep, a protective circle, bird blood inscriptions, and a symbolic sword sacrifice. The text compares this practice to the ars notoria and medieval exempla like Caesarius of Heisterbach’s schoolboy story.
key_passages: ‘Apolin, Maraloth, Berith, I, so-and-so, exorcise and conjure you … to send me a certain spirit who is expert in teaching of all the sciences…'
When this has been said, the kings will depart and the master alone will remain, and will say to you, ‘Arise, behold your master.’
The circle required for this first experiment is a single band inscribed within a square. Inscribed in and around the band are the names of seven spirits and the four cardinal directions.
One might suppose that the magician conceives the sword further as a symbol for the power brought by his newly gained knowledge…

...the ars notoria did not call upon demonic aid, and ostensibly revolved around the recitation of devotions to the Virgin and to unfallen angels, its techniques were untraditional and superstitious...
